artifacts.content:
Hi Karen,

I wanted to write this down while it’s still fresh, so you have the whole picture and I don’t forget bits when we talk.

When the aide never showed on 02/10/2026 for that 8–9 a.m. visit, it brought back everything from that awful fall in October 2024 in the bathroom here at 4138 Willow Creek. I keep seeing myself on that cold floor for almost 40 minutes, calling out until Mrs. Reynolds from 3C finally heard me and called 911. Dr. Patel at Bronson Methodist and Daniel have both told me over and over not to shower alone anymore, and now I really understand why.

That morning I waited until about 9:15, called the PACE main line, sat on hold, and no one ever called me back. I tried to get into the tub with the shower chair and nearly slipped again. My heart was pounding like it did after Robert’s funeral at St. Augustine’s in March 2014, when Dr. Harris first put me on that anxiety pill. I didn’t even take it until almost 10:30 because I was so rattled.

By the time you left the Portage City Treasurer’s office around 11:45 and burned 3 hours of your PTO to come here, I was exhausted and ashamed. I hate that my arthritis, blood pressure, and all this business with near falls keeps pulling you away from work and making you worry about my safety.

Thank you for coming anyway. I just want you to know how scared I was and why I’m double-checking the clock now every time an aide is even a little late.

Love,
Mom

Dear Daniel,

Thank you again for calling on 02-18-2026 and taking the time to explain what happened with the missed 02/10 visit and what changes you and the Home Care Coordinator are putting in place. It reminded me a lot of when I was at Bronson Methodist Hospital with the hip fracture back in March 2022 and Adult Protective Services got involved for a bit because Karen told them I’d tried to shower alone and fell twice in one week. I don’t ever want to get back to that point.

I do feel somewhat better now that you’ve promised weekly check-ins for a while and the extra grab bar and non-slip mat for the bathroom here at Willow Creek. After last summer (July–August 2025) when my anxiety got so bad that Dr. Patel at the outpatient psychiatry clinic on Gull Road doubled my sertraline to 100 mg and added lorazepam for those panic attacks in the shower, I’m afraid another missed visit could trigger that all over again.

Karen and I also talked after your call. I told her that if there’s another situation where an aide just doesn’t come and no one calls, she has my permission to use the durable power of attorney paperwork we signed with Attorney Mark Ellison downtown in November 2023. We agreed she could step in more strongly on medical and financial decisions then, including possibly cancelling PACE and using my remaining savings for assisted living if it comes to that.

For now, I’d rather stay here with PACE support than move to a place like Park Village Pines, as long as the aides keep coming when they’re supposed to.

Sincerely,
Margaret L. Henderson

Personal health & feelings log – 02/10/2026

Woke up 6:30 a.m., knees stiff and shoulders sore. Light breakfast (tea, toast, half banana). Aide was supposed to come 8–9 a.m. for special Tuesday visit before podiatry.

8:15 – No aide. Watched parking lot from chair.
9:15 – Still no one. Called PACE main line, on hold ~11 minutes. Told receptionist (Denise? Danielle?) that I needed help bathing and dressing before my afternoon appointment with Dr. Lisa Monroe at Bronson Foot & Ankle Clinic on Westnedge. She said she’d “look into it” and call back. No call.

Tried to shower with rollator parked by door, shower chair and grab bars. Nearly slipped stepping into tub. Sat hard on edge; right hip hurt, heart racing. Skipped compression stockings because I couldn’t manage them alone.

Missed my usual 8:30 anxiety pill from Dr. Harris at the PACE clinic. Didn’t take it until almost 10:30. Felt jittery and panicky, like right after Robert’s funeral at St. Augustine’s in March 2014.

At Bronson Foot & Ankle, told Dr. Monroe I almost didn’t come because I felt dirty and ashamed. Cried in the exam room for about 10 minutes while she was looking at my toenail fungus and circulation problems. She was kind, but it was embarrassing.

Evening: ankles more swollen (no stockings). Called Thomas around 7:30 p.m. from here at Willow Creek. Told him I was scared about living alone with arthritis and blood pressure and that I almost slipped in the tub. Asked whether I should think again about Maple Grove Senior Living on Oakland Drive (we toured Nov 2025 – $4,200/month, would mean selling the Grand Rapids house with Michael).

Hi Michael,

I got the formal letter yesterday (02-19-2026) about that missed visit on the 10th, and I wanted to talk it through with you.

I called you from the apartment after I read it. I told you I’m satisfied with the plan as long as they keep the extra safety pieces in place and don’t start cutting back visits again. You remember what happened early 2024 when I tried to reduce help to save a bit so I could slip Thomas some money toward his student loan payments – I ended up falling in the bathroom and needing six full weeks of in-home physical therapy from Bronson at Home. I don’t want to repeat that.

This whole episode stirred up a lot. When the aide never showed on 02/10 and no one called me back, I ended up phoning Karen at the Portage City Treasurer’s Department around 11:45 a.m. She left work early again and used 3 hours of PTO to come here because she was afraid I might fall like I did in 2024. She’s worried about her job; that’s the third time in a year my health has pulled her out of work.

I also talked with Thomas that night around 7:30. I told him I was scared to keep living alone here at Willow Creek and even brought up Maple Grove Senior Living on Oakland Drive again – the place we toured with him in November 2025 that runs about $4,200 a month. I said I’d probably have to sell the Grand Rapids house you and I co-own to afford it, which I’d rather not do unless we really have to.

For now, I’m willing to stay put with PACE as long as they “stick to what they promised.”

Love,
Mom

metadata.subject: PACE letter and what comes next

artifacts.content:
Notebook – Planning & legal reminders

Date: 2026-02-21
Location: Kitchen table, Willow Creek

1. Power of Attorney
- In November 2023, Karen and I signed durable power of attorney paperwork with Attorney Mark Ellison at his office downtown Kalamazoo.
- On 02-18-2026, after the follow-up call with Daniel Morales and the Home Care Coordinator about the missed 02/10 aide visit, I told Karen that if there is another “no-show and no call,” she has my blessing to use that POA to step in for medical and financial decisions.
- This could include cancelling PACE if it stops feeling safe and using my remaining ~$18,000 in savings toward assisted living.

2. Assisted living possibilities
- Maple Grove Senior Living on Oakland Drive: toured with Thomas in November 2025. Nice, but about $4,200/month. Would likely require selling the Grand Rapids house I co-own with Michael.
- Park Village Pines in Kalamazoo: Karen’s preferred option if I can’t safely stay here, especially because of my history – hip fracture at Bronson Methodist in March 2022 (two shower falls that week, APS briefly involved) and the October 2024 wrist fracture after the bathroom fall.

3. Mental health
- Anxiety worsened July–August 2025; Dr. Patel at the outpatient psychiatry clinic on Gull Road increased sertraline to 100 mg and added lorazepam for panic attacks in the shower.
- Need to keep up with weekly check-ins Daniel promised so I don’t slide back there.

Reminder to self: I want to stay here as long as it’s truly safe, but I don’t want Karen and Michael blindsided if things change quickly.

Event: Weekly check-in call – PACE
Date: Wednesday, 2026-02-25
Time: 2:30–3:00 p.m.
Location: Home – 4138 Willow Creek Dr, Apt 2B (phone call)
Attendees: Me, Daniel Morales (MSW social worker), Home Care Coordinator (name TBD)

Notes to discuss during call:

1. How I’ve been feeling since the missed aide visit on 02/10/2026.
- Still thinking about that October 2024 bathroom fall here at Willow Creek when I broke my wrist and lay on the floor for nearly 40 minutes before Mrs. Reynolds in 3C heard me and called 911. Need them to understand that when someone doesn’t show, I’m back in that headspace.
- Remind them Dr. Patel at Bronson Methodist and Dr. Harris at the PACE clinic both told me not to shower alone anymore because of my history (hip fracture at Bronson in March 2022, two shower falls in one week, APS involved briefly).

2. Anxiety and medications
- Tell them about how my anxiety flared on 02/10: delayed taking my morning pill until almost 10:30, felt like the days after Robert’s funeral at St. Augustine’s in March 2014.
- Ask if the weekly calls will continue at least through spring, especially given last summer (July–August 2025) when Dr. Patel at the Gull Road psychiatry clinic had to raise sertraline to 100 mg and add lorazepam for panic attacks in the shower.

3. Future planning
- Clarify that Karen has POA from November 2023 with Mark Ellison and what happens if there’s another missed visit.
- Briefly mention my worries about possibly needing assisted living (Maple Grove or Park Village Pines) if things don’t stay stable.

metadata.title: Weekly check-in call – Daniel / Home Care Coordinator

artifacts.content:
Hi Emily dear,

Thank you again for stopping by last weekend to do your laundry and fuss over my plants. They perk up after you visit, and so do I.

You asked how I’m really doing after that whole mess on 02/10, so here it is. When the aide never came and no one called, I ended up telling my podiatrist, Dr. Monroe at Bronson Foot & Ankle on Westnedge, that I almost didn’t come because I felt dirty and ashamed. I actually broke down crying in the exam room for about 10 minutes while she was looking at my poor toenail fungus and checking my circulation. She was very gentle, but I still felt silly afterward.

Since then, Daniel and the Home Care Coordinator have been better about checking in. They called on 02-18-2026 and promised weekly calls for a while. I told them I feel a little safer with that, especially after last July–August when my anxiety got so bad that Dr. Patel at the psychiatry clinic on Gull Road had to double my sertraline to 100 mg and add lorazepam because I kept having panic attacks in the shower.

I also told Mom (your Aunt Karen) that if PACE misses more visits, she can use the power of attorney we signed with Attorney Mark Ellison back in November 2023 to step in more. I don’t love the idea, but I’d rather have her help me make decisions than let things slide until there’s another fall.

Enough heavy stuff. How are your classes at Western going? Remember, you can always bring your friends over for a pot of soup and a quiet place to study.

Love you,
Grandma
